name: Pseudomonas putida KT2440
shortDescription: A certified safety-strain soil bacterium with exceptional solvent/stress tolerance and diverse metabolism. The premier chassis for environmental biosensing of pollutants and heavy metals in soil and water.

safety.biocontainment: Well-developed containment circuits; the first Gram-negative soil bacterium certified as a safe cloning host (HV1).
safety.bslSource: ABSA Risk Group 1; KT2440 is a certified host-vector biosafety (HV1) strain (US Federal Register, 1982).
safety.notes: Rifampicin-resistant plasmid-cured derivative of P. putida mt-2; metabolically versatile, robust outdoors.

engineering.crisprTools: dCas9/CRISPRi and CRISPR base-editing established in P. putida.

provenance.source: NCBI Taxonomy; SEVA; HV1 certification (US Federal Register 1982)
